title: Gendo super senior
wikitext:
[]: Gendo: super senior?             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Aug 24, 2010 5:34 am Noting the 10-year age difference between Gendo and Yui, I have to wonder just how the hell they were both in college at the same time. Did he get a late start on college, maybe doing some military service first? Did he somehow fail to test in multiple times? Was he a complete slacker who simply took that long to finish his degree? Or was he maybe a 'professional student', working on a second or third unrelated degree just because he could? Wild speculation thread, engage!

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Tue Aug 24, 2010 7:09 am Presumably he was a graduate student teaching one of Yui's classes. (...and they made an arrangement to ensure she would pass the class?)

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Aug 24, 2010 8:55 am Research.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Tue Aug 24, 2010 12:20 pm He just wanted to get sum. College chicks, you know.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Aug 26, 2010 3:58 am drinian wrote: Presumably he was a graduate student teaching one of Yui's classes.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Aug 26, 2010 9:02 am Waited a few years before entering college?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Thu Aug 26, 2010 11:03 am He stretched a fuck-win gap year into 10?

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Thu Aug 26, 2010 8:22 pm There are people who go to college at various points in their life. Several months ago, I had read about a woman who finally got her degree in art history at the ripe old age of 88. I'm guessing Gendo had already established himself in some career, most likely of a less than savory nature, and was working towards a college diploma simply for the prestige points. Then again, he does engage in some degree of technobabble: "restart the process from step 307". And just because Keel and He criticize the scientists at the beginning of the director's cut, doesn't mean they aren't scientists of some sort, only that they are commenting on the prevalent attitudes of their fellow scientists.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Aug 26, 2010 8:33 pm Sun Stealer wrote: Then again, he does engage in some degree of technobabble: "restart the process from step 307". And just because Keel and He criticize the scientists at the beginning of the director's cut, doesn't mean they aren't scientists of some sort, only that they are commenting on the prevalent attitudes of their fellow scientists.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Thu Aug 26, 2010 11:17 pm It's not only that. Yui alone isn't responsible for the Eva project, it is her creation but she died during one of the first activation tests, it was him and Fujutsuki that are mostly responsible for compleating the Eva project. Gendo was also part of the Katsuragi expedition to the north pole where Adam was which implies some sort of scientific involvement. Also, having an entire instrumentality project of his own would requiere the scientific now how to pull off.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Aug 27, 2010 12:05 am So maybe he landed some cushy gov job (like a tax auditor or something equally unpleasant to others) and was going back to school to get a higher degree to qualify for something further up the chain?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Oct 25, 2010 1:36 am Didn't the government Guys in ep. 1 adress him as Dr., or as that just a translation error?

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Oct 25, 2010 5:14 pm He belonged to the faculty staff, or possibly a plot hole.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Oct 25, 2010 6:34 pm Perhaps he had done prison time? His life before meeting Fuyutsuki and Yui seemed to be a boisterous one, at best. After getting out of prison he decided to get a higher education.

[]:             Quote Post by beaverteeth92 » Mon Oct 25, 2010 9:36 pm It's not relevant. But if it really matters, he was probably a grad student helping Yui with Fuyutsuki advising both of them.
